_meta.description: Pew Research Center knowledge quiz questions with nationally representative % correct data and full answer choices. For use in crowd-modeling trivia game.

schema_notes.pct_correct: Percentage of nationally representative sample who answered correctly (integer). Excludes 'Not sure' — denominator is full sample including those who answered 'Not sure'.
schema_notes.answer_choices: All original multiple-choice options EXCLUDING 'Not sure'. First item is always the correct answer. Array is ordered as shown in topline (original display order was randomized per respondent).
schema_notes.sample_size: n for the specific survey wave
schema_notes.pct_source: Always 'pew' in this file — real nationally representative data
schema_notes.calibration_tier: 'opener' = suitable for Q1 (mid-difficulty, engaging, not obscure); 'mid' = standard round questions; 'harder' = low % correct, use later in round
schema_notes.flagged: true = review before including. See flag_reason.
schema_notes.image_dependent: true = original question used a photo/image/graph as part of the stimulus. % correct is from that image version.

sources.report_title: What Americans Know and Don't Know About Science
What Americans Know About AI, Cybersecurity and Big Tech
What Do Americans Know About International Affairs?

sources.panel: American Trends Panel Wave 44 + Ipsos KnowledgePanel (supplemental)

sources.report_title: What Americans Know About Their Government

questions.question: Oil, natural gas, and coal are examples of what?

A scientist tests a new ear infection medication: all patients used 10 drops daily for two weeks, and all healed. What change would MOST improve this study's ability to test whether the medication works?
answer_choices: Create a second group of patients with ear infections who receive no ear drops
Create a second group of patients with ear infections who use 15 drops a day
Have participants use ear drops for only one week
Have participants put ear drops in both their infected ear and their healthy ear
questions.correct_answer: Create a second group of patients with ear infections who receive no ear drops

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of genetic engineering?
answer_choices: Inserting a gene into plants that makes them resistant to insects
Growing a whole plant from a single cell
Finding the sequences of bases in plant DNA
Attaching the root of one type of plant to the stem of another type of plant
questions.correct_answer: Inserting a gene into plants that makes them resistant to insects

questions.question: What is the main cause of seasons on Earth?
answer_choices: The tilt of the Earth's axis in relation to the sun
The distance between the Earth and the sun
The speed at which the Earth rotates around the sun
Changes in the amount of energy coming from the sun
questions.correct_answer: The tilt of the Earth's axis in relation to the sun

questions.question: A car's startup time has dramatically increased. One possible explanation is that the computer is running out of memory. In scientific terms, this explanation is a…
Many diseases have an incubation period. Which of the following best describes what an incubation period is?
answer_choices: The period during which someone has an infection but is not showing symptoms
The recovery period after being sick
The effect of a disease on babies
The period during which someone builds up immunity to a disease
questions.correct_answer: The period during which someone has an infection but is not showing symptoms

questions.question: When large areas of forest are cleared for farming, which of the following occurs?
An antacid relieves an overly acidic stomach because the main components of antacids are…
Which of these is a major concern about the overuse of antibiotics?
answer_choices: It can lead to antibiotic-resistant bacteria
There will be an antibiotic shortage
Antibiotics will get into the water system
questions.correct_answer: It can lead to antibiotic-resistant bacteria

questions.question: A car travels at a constant speed of 40 miles per hour. How far does it travel in 45 minutes?
What is the hottest layer of the Earth?
questions.note: Original question showed a labeled diagram of Earth's layers. Answer choices reconstructed from report context; topline PDF not retrieved.

questions.question: Ocean tides are primarily created by what?
answer_choices: The gravitational pull of the moon
questions.correct_answer: The gravitational pull of the moon
questions.flag_reason: Answer choices not confirmed from topline — reconstructed from report context. Verify before use.

questions.question: Which of these is the study of how the positions of stars and planets can influence human behavior — astronomy or astrology?
questions.note: This was a two-option question in the original survey.

questions.question: A light-year is a measure of what?
questions.flag_reason: Answer choices not confirmed from topline — reconstructed from report context. Verify before use.

questions.question: What property of a sound wave determines its loudness?
questions.flag_reason: Answer choices not confirmed from topline — reconstructed from report context. Verify before use.

questions.question: Compared to sea level, does water boil at a higher or lower temperature at high altitudes?
questions.flag_reason: Answer choices not confirmed from topline — reconstructed from report context. Verify before use.

questions.question: If a website uses cookies, what can it do?
answer_choices: Track your visits and activity on the site
Protect your computer from getting viruses
Look at files saved on your computer
questions.correct_answer: Track your visits and activity on the site

questions.question: What is a 'deepfake'?
answer_choices: A seemingly real, computer-generated image, video, or audio of something that did not occur
A text-based chatbot that tricks people into thinking they are talking to a person
A system that records and protects information online
A program that makes it look like someone is using the internet from a different location
questions.correct_answer: A seemingly real, computer-generated image, video, or audio of something that did not occur

questions.question: In 2021, Facebook changed its company name to what?
Which of the following passwords is the most secure?
How do large language models like ChatGPT generate responses to questions?
answer_choices: By creating responses based on word patterns and relationships previously learned from text on the internet
By randomly generating sentences that reorganize keywords from the submitted question
By searching the internet and summarizing what the top results say
By using pre-written responses that plug in keywords from the question
questions.correct_answer: By creating responses based on word patterns and relationships previously learned from text on the internet

questions.question: In the U.S., websites are prohibited from collecting personal data from children under what age without a parent's consent?
questions.note: This question had 5 numeric answer choices in the original survey, not 4.

questions.question: Does the United States have a national privacy law that sets common standards for what companies can do with all data they collect?
Which of the following countries is NOT a member of NATO?
What is the dominant religion in Latin America?
What currency does the '€' symbol represent?
questions.note: Original showed the symbol as an image; the symbol itself can be rendered in the question text.

questions.question: The USMCA trade agreement replaced which previous trade agreement?
After the U.S. Embassy in Israel moved in 2018, where is it now located?
What is the capital of Afghanistan?
Which African country is known for both having the largest population and being one of the continent's largest oil producers?
Which region of China has the highest per capita population of Muslims?
Which Bible figure is most closely associated with leading the Exodus from Egypt?
Which Bible figure is most closely associated with killing an enemy by slinging a stone?
According to the Christian Gospels, who delivered the Sermon on the Mount?
What is an atheist?
answer_choices: Someone who does NOT believe in God
Someone who is unsure whether God exists
Someone who believes in multiple gods
questions.correct_answer: Someone who does NOT believe in God

questions.question: What is an agnostic?
answer_choices: Someone who is unsure whether God exists
Someone who does NOT believe in God
Someone who believes in multiple gods
questions.correct_answer: Someone who is unsure whether God exists

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the Ten Commandments?
answer_choices: Do unto others as you would have them do unto you
questions.correct_answer: Do unto others as you would have them do unto you

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the Jewish holiday of Rosh Hashana?
answer_choices: Day of Atonement for one's sins
Festival during which candles are lit for eight nights
Celebration of the end of the Torah reading cycle

questions.question: In the Muslim tradition, believers have a religious obligation to make a pilgrimage to Islam's holiest city. Which city is that?
What is the religion of most people in Thailand?
What is the religion of most people in Indonesia?
In the Christian tradition, what does Easter Sunday commemorate?
answer_choices: Last Supper of Jesus and his apostles

questions.question: Which Bible figure is most closely associated with willingness to sacrifice his son in obedience to God?
Which Biblical figure is most closely associated with saving Jews from murder by appealing to the king?
Which of the following best describes Catholic teaching about the bread and wine used for Communion?
answer_choices: They actually become the body and blood of Jesus Christ
They are symbols of the body and blood of Jesus Christ
questions.correct_answer: They actually become the body and blood of Jesus Christ

questions.question: According to Catholic tradition, what is purgatory?
answer_choices: Where the souls of those who have died undergo purification before entering heaven
An offering Catholics make during the sacrament of confession
The purification process Catholics undertake during seasons like Advent and Lent
Where the souls of evildoers go for eternal punishment after they die
questions.correct_answer: Where the souls of those who have died undergo purification before entering heaven

questions.question: Which religious tradition is most closely associated with yoga?
Which religious tradition is most closely associated with Kabbalah?
Which of the following is one of Buddhism's four 'noble truths'?
answer_choices: The truth that every living being has an immortal soul
The truth that the Buddha was perfect and free from sin

questions.question: What was the religion of Joseph Smith?
What was the religion of Maimonides, the influential medieval scholar?
Which of the following texts is most closely associated with the Hindu tradition?
On which day of the week does the Jewish Sabbath begin?
Which of the following best describes the Christian doctrine of the Trinity?
answer_choices: There is one God in three persons (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit)
There are three patriarchs (Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob)
The coming of Christ was foretold by three prophets (Elijah, Ezekiel, and Zechariah)
There are three Gods (Father, Mother, and Son)
questions.correct_answer: There is one God in three persons (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit)

questions.question: According to the Bible, where did Jesus live during much of his childhood and young adulthood?
Which of these religious groups traditionally teaches that salvation comes through faith alone?
What does the U.S. Constitution say about religion as it relates to federal officeholders?
answer_choices: No religious test shall be required for holding office
They shall be sworn in using the Holy Bible
They shall affirm that all men are endowed by their Creator with certain unalienable rights
The Constitution does not say anything one way or the other about this
questions.correct_answer: No religious test shall be required for holding office

questions.question: What is Ramadan?
answer_choices: A Jewish prayer for the dead

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the teaching of the 'prosperity gospel'?
answer_choices: Those of strong faith will be blessed by God with financial success and good health
It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God
To whom much is given, much is expected
God's blessing is given to the poor in spirit, who shall store up treasures in heaven
questions.correct_answer: Those of strong faith will be blessed by God with financial success and good health

questions.question: What percentage of U.S. adults are Jewish — less than 5%, about 1 in 10, about 1 in 4, or half or more?
What percentage of U.S. adults are Muslim — less than 5%, about 1 in 10, about 1 in 4, or half or more?
When a website has a privacy policy, it means that the site has done which of the following?
answer_choices: Created a contract between itself and its users about how it will use their data
Agreed not to share its users' personal information with third parties
Agreed to adhere to federal guidelines about deceptive advertising practices
Agreed not to retain any personally identifying information about its users
questions.correct_answer: Created a contract between itself and its users about how it will use their data

questions.question: What does it mean when a website URL begins with 'https://' rather than 'http://' (without the 's')?
answer_choices: Information entered into the site is encrypted
The content on the site is safe for children
The site is only accessible to people in certain countries
The site has been verified as trustworthy
questions.correct_answer: Information entered into the site is encrypted

questions.question: Where might someone encounter a phishing scam?
answer_choices: All of the above — email, social media, text messages, and websites
questions.correct_answer: All of the above — email, social media, text messages, and websites
questions.note: Original had all four platforms as individual options plus 'All of the above.' Adapted to preserve the correct answer concept without revealing the answer structure.

questions.question: The term 'net neutrality' describes the principle that…
answer_choices: Internet service providers should treat all traffic on their networks equally
Social media platforms must give equal visibility to conservative and liberal points of view
Online advertisers cannot post ads for housing or jobs visible only to people of a certain race
The government cannot censor online speech
questions.correct_answer: Internet service providers should treat all traffic on their networks equally

questions.question: In a U.S. presidential election, who chooses the next president if the vote in the Electoral College is tied?
Which of the following rights is guaranteed by the First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution?
answer_choices: The right to freedom of religion
questions.correct_answer: The right to freedom of religion

questions.question: Which of the following determines how many representatives a state gets in the U.S. House of Representatives?
answer_choices: The number of people who live in the state
The square mile area of the state
The number of voting-age citizens in the state
How much people in the state pay in federal taxes
questions.correct_answer: The number of people who live in the state

questions.question: How many years are in one full term of office for a U.S. senator?
When a justice is confirmed to the U.S. Supreme Court, how long are they appointed for?
answer_choices: Until a president decides to replace them

questions.question: In which part of the federal government is there a rule requiring a 60% supermajority vote to end a filibuster?
What is the religion of most people in Ethiopia?
